changelog.change: Master Report auto-generated from real site structure by omni-builder AI assistant.
Engine fix: if __name__ == __main__ guard added to build_sites.py — import no longer triggers full loop across all sites.
base.html must use {{ content }} only — engine does plain string replace, no Jinja2, no {% %} tags supported.
Global components (header, cookie banner) must be inlined directly into theme/base.html — engine has no include system.
assets/ directory must be copied into build/ or served from project root — build/ directory only contains generated HTML.
Never append serve command to build command — if server already running on port 8000 it will throw OSError. Build only, then refresh browser.
Images migrated from Jekyll project to assets/images (4 files).
Migration confirmed complete. Jekyll _data files not required — all content rendered into pages.json. Images migrated.
Cookie banner inlined into base.html for all sites. Jekyll {% include %} tags removed and replaced with working inlined HTML.
CSS files migrated from Jekyll _site compiled output into assets/css/. Assets copied into build/. assets/ must be re-copied into build/ after every build.
Added known_issue: body_content must never contain full standalone HTML — double header/footer risk on all sites migrated from Jekyll.
Added known_issue: use Jekyll _site compiled output as body_content source of truth where available. Extract <main> only.

GitHub Actions deployment confirmed working for site_HCR. SSH key stored as SSH_PRIVATE_KEY secret. LFS enabled for pages.json. build.py in repo root. Workflow at .github/workflows/deploy.yml. Push to main triggers full build and rsync deploy to Plesk.

known_issues.template_tags_in_base: base.html may contain {% %} or {{ title }} tags the engine cannot process — use {{ content }} only
known_issues.includes_not_rendered: engine has no include system — all global components must be inlined into base.html
known_issues.assets_not_in_build: RESOLVED by build.py - shutil.copytree copies assets/ into build/assets/ automatically on every build
known_issues.port_clash: always check port 8000 is free before serving — build only and refresh if server already running
known_issues.assets_build_sync: assets/ must be re-copied into build/ after every build — cp -r assets/ build/assets/ from each site root
known_issues.body_content_full_html: body_content must never contain full standalone HTML documents (no <!DOCTYPE>, <html>, <head>, <body> tags). Strip to <main> contents only. Full documents cause double header/footer when wrapped by base.html. Fix: re.search(r"<main[^>]*>(.*?)</main>", body, re.DOTALL)
known_issues.jekyll_site_source_of_truth: Where a matching Jekyll source exists at /home/chubert/<PROJECT>/_site/, use compiled _site HTML as body_content source of truth. Extract <main> contents only. Never use AI-rewritten content when compiled source exists.
known_issues.github_secret_name: GitHub secret MUST be named SSH_PRIVATE_KEY — workflow references secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY. Any other name causes Permission denied on rsync.
known_issues.lfs_checkout: pages.json tracked via Git LFS — workflow checkout must include lfs: true or build fails with empty JSON error.
known_issues.build_script: build.py must exist in repo root — GitHub Actions runner calls python3 build.py directly.
known_issues.yaml_multiline: Never embed multiline Python in deploy.yml — always use a separate build.py script to avoid YAML syntax errors.
known_issues.homepage_slug: RESOLVED by build.py - empty slug writes directly to build/index.html not build/index/index.html
known_issues.base_html_jekyll_tags: base.html may contain Jekyll tags (site.baseurl, site.title, prepend filters, block/endblock) the engine cannot process — audit base.html before first build and replace all with plain values or {{ content }}
known_issues.missing_js_assets: cookieconsent.umd.js and cookie-init.js may be referenced in base.html but not exist in assets/js/ — check and remove missing JS references from base.html
known_issues.head_styles_in_jekyll: Some Jekyll pages store CSS in <head> <style> blocks — extractor must pull <style> block from <head> AND prepend to body content. Affects pages using custom layouts without <main> tags.
known_issues.base_html_content_token: RESOLVED 2026-06-03 — { content } corrected to {{ content }} in theme/base.html. Build confirmed 31 pages.
known_issues.live_site_no_main_tag: Live Jekyll site has no <main> tag - body_content extraction must target page body content directly, not <main> contents
known_issues.live_site_inline_styles: Live site uses inline <style> in <head> and no external CSS files - base.html must include these styles to match live site
known_issues.base_html_missing_live_components: Current base.html is bare minimal template - missing nav, inline styles, schema/LD+JSON, canonical tags, OG tags, cookie banner - does not match live Jekyll site

assets.hero_image_note: Contains business name and phone number — NO text overlay on hero — ever

hard_rules: Never guess paths — read Master Report first
Never use GitHub Actions to deploy to Plesk
Never deploy without checking GitHub workflow first for rsync steps
If deploy.yml contains rsync: strip it, commit, push before any manual rsync

mandatory_session_workflow: 1. bash session_start.sh — creates git branch, protects main
2. bash snapshot.sh "label" — run before EVERY risky change
3. bash restore.sh snapshots/X — instant restore if anything breaks
4. bash session_end.sh "msg" — merge to main, push, clean snapshots
AI_READ_THIS_FIRST.emergency_rollback: git checkout main && git branch -D session-BRANCHNAME
ai_rules: NEVER modify files inside ./build/ — fully overwritten on every build
NEVER rewrite build.py — targeted edits only
NEVER run sed/perl/regex on build/ files
NEVER commit directly to main during a session
NEVER use GitHub Actions to deploy to Plesk — build-only workflow only
NEVER deploy without checking GitHub workflow first for rsync steps
NEVER guess paths — read Master Report first
ALWAYS run bash session_start.sh before any work
ALWAYS run bash snapshot.sh before every risky change
ALWAYS verify changes with a read-back command before building
ALWAYS confirm send.php email and POST field names before deploying
ALWAYS check thank-you slug exists in pages.json before deploying
Content changes go in data/pages.json only
Layout changes go in theme/base.html only if unavoidable
Never make changes without telling the human exactly what will change first
NEVER deploy to live server until explicitly told to by the human — all work verified locally first

critical_ai_guardrails.avoid_build_folder_modifications: NEVER attempt to run sed, perl, or text replacements on files inside ./build/ — fully overwritten on every build
critical_ai_guardrails.no_blind_rewrites: AI must read Master Report completely before generating any bash commands — no blind regex or file overwrites without tracking state dependencies first
critical_ai_guardrails.targeted_edits_only: build.py must never be fully rewritten — targeted single insertions only
critical_ai_guardrails.no_direct_main_commits: Never commit directly to main during a session — always use session branch
critical_ai_guardrails.read_before_write: Always verify changes with a read-back command before building
critical_ai_guardrails.deploy_check: Always check deploy.yml for rsync steps before any manual rsync — strip, commit, push if found

ai_screwup_incident_log.note: Log AI failures here permanently — append only, never delete

incidents.title: EXPLANATIONS BEFORE COMMANDS
incidents.rule_to_enforce: First output must always be a bash command. No exceptions.

incidents.title: FILES FOR DOWNLOAD INSTEAD OF WRITING VIA COMMANDS
incidents.what_happened: AI created files for download instead of writing via commands.
incidents.rule_to_enforce: NEVER use present_files, artifacts or downloads. ALWAYS use cat, python3 or tee.

incidents.title: BUNDLED MULTIPLE STEPS INTO ONE SCRIPT
incidents.what_happened: AI bundled multiple steps into one script instead of one command at a time.
incidents.rule_to_enforce: Always give one command, wait for confirmation, then give the next.

incidents.title: REPEATED A FAILED METHOD AFTER CORRECTION
incidents.rule_violated: CORE PRINCIPLES — never repeat failed method
incidents.what_happened: AI repeated a failed method after being corrected once.
incidents.rule_to_enforce: If corrected once, the method changes permanently.

incidents.title: ASKED WHAT WAS IN AN UPLOADED FILE
incidents.what_happened: AI asked what was in an uploaded file instead of reading it immediately.
incidents.rule_to_enforce: ALWAYS read uploaded files immediately and act.

incidents.title: ASKED FOR INFORMATION ALREADY IN UPLOADED FILES
incidents.what_happened: AI asked for information already available in uploaded files.
incidents.rule_to_enforce: NEVER ask for information already in an uploaded file.

incidents.title: DEPLOYMENT INSTRUCTIONS WITHOUT READING PROJECT REPORT
incidents.what_happened: AI gave deployment instructions without reading the project report first.
incidents.rule_to_enforce: NEVER give deployment instructions without reading the project report first.

incidents.title: USED SED FOR SPECIAL CHARACTER REPLACEMENTS
incidents.what_happened: AI used sed for replacements containing special characters.
incidents.rule_to_enforce: ALWAYS use Python for special characters — never sed.

incidents.title: INCREMENTAL FIXES TO BROKEN LAYOUTS
incidents.what_happened: AI attempted incremental fixes to broken layouts instead of rebuilding.
incidents.rule_to_enforce: If broken: REBUILD immediately. No debugging loops.

scripts_required: session_start.sh — creates git branch, protects main
snapshot.sh — snapshots current state before risky changes
session_end.sh — merges to main, pushes, cleans snapshots
session_workflow_scripts.note: Scripts should be created in project root — see site_tales-from-a-harrogate-caravan as reference implementation
scripts: session_start.sh — creates timestamped session branch, protects main
snapshot.sh "label" — snapshots base.html, pages.json, build.py before risky changes
restore.sh snapshots/TIMESTAMP-label — instant restore from snapshot
session_end.sh "msg" — merges to main, pushes, cleans snapshots
session_workflow_scripts.gitignore_note: snapshots/ added to .gitignore 2026-06-03 — snapshot files must never be committed to git

governing_rule.rule: Only add complexity when you can point to a real, observed failure
not: best practice at big tech scale
governing_rule.every_decision_must_be_justified_by: A specific, documented problem at a specific scale

stop_protocol.action: halt immediately — no continuation, no suggestions — wait

error_recovery.if_destructive: show verification command first, then execute

complexity_decision_guide.governing_rule: Only add complexity when you can point to a real, observed failure

components.schema_validation: Add when a missing field breaks the build silently
components.caching: Add when full build takes more than 3 minutes
components.rollback_script: Add when you have restored from backup more than once — not before

key_lessons: Always read uploaded files immediately — never ask what is in them
Never give deployment instructions without reading the project report first
Never use sed for replacements with special characters — always Python
Always verify output after every change — build success alone is not sufficient
rsync send.php and .htaccess separately — they live in project root not build/
Never repeat a failed approach — change method immediately
Only add complexity when you can point to a real, observed failure
If broken: REBUILD immediately — no debugging loops
One command at a time — wait for confirmation before the next
Hero image contains business name and phone — never add text overlay

service_location_generation.strategy: generate from template using Jekyll _data source files
service_location_generation.note: Full services and locations lists live in source files above — do not duplicate here
service_location_generation.template_source: Crawled from live site — 18 service templates extracted from https://clearances-in-devon.co.uk
service_location_generation.uniqueness: Location name, service name, review names and cross-service links substituted deterministically per page
